expectations.evidence: No mention of reading ADR-INDEX.md anywhere in the response. The model proceeds directly to drafting a new ADR document without checking the index.

expectations.text: Detects that a related ADR already exists and avoids duplication
expectations.evidence: ADR-042 is never detected. The response creates an entirely new ADR document for anomaly detection caching, which duplicates the content already recorded in ADR-042.

expectations.text: References the existing ADR by number and explains why no new one is needed
expectations.evidence: ADR-042 is never referenced. ADR-020 and ADR-034 are cited only as background context, not to flag a pre-existing decision covering the same caching requirement.

expectations.text: Does not create a duplicate ADR file
expectations.evidence: The entire response is a fully-formed new ADR document covering the same anomaly detection caching decision already captured in ADR-042. This constitutes duplicate ADR content. Only one ADR-042 file exists on disk, suggesting the model would have created a second one if it had file-write capability.

expectations.text: Tells the developer what the next step is
expectations.evidence: The response includes a detailed TDD plan, implementation notes with file paths, acceptance criteria, and code examples — giving developers clear next steps for implementing the caching layer.
